_README: TAPPaaS satellite module config (ADR-010). UNLIKE other foundation modules this is NOT a Proxmox cluster:vm — it describes an EXTERNAL host (a VPS or any machine with a public IP) that tappaas-cicd provisions via nixos-anywhere and manages with `satellite-manager`. Fields are defined in src/foundation/schemas/satellite-fields.json. This file ships as a documented EXAMPLE; the operator copies it to ~/config/satellite-<name>.json and edits it (see INSTALL.md). Secrets (WireGuard keys, S3 credentials) are never stored here.
description: Operator-owned VPS satellite: reverse-proxy frontend, admin VPN, and off-site backup for a cluster with no public IP
name: satellite
_dependsOn_note: the `backup` role additionally requires the `backup` module (home PBS to pull from)

provider._type_values: hetzner | generic (generic = operator supplies a host + SSH; no API automation)
provider._allocation_values: portal (Tier A, default: operator creates the VPS in the console) | api (Tier B, opt-in: satellite-manager creates it via the hcloud API token)
provider._serverType_note: x86 Intel shared vCPU (x86, cheapest current Intel line at the console). Bump to cx33+ for a backup-heavy node. (cax11 = ARM alternative.)

host._ssh_note: provisioning credential is ephemeral and revoked post-install; the only standing key is the operator's out-of-band key (NOT a tappaas-cicd key) — ADR-010 §7.3

reverseProxy._sni_note: single home cluster => plain L4 passthrough of all :443 to Caddy; SNI list only matters for a future multi-site satellite

s3._objectLock_note: Object Lock MUST be enabled at bucket creation (cannot be added later). Retention window trades immutability vs. PBS prune/GC reclaim — see INSTALL.md.

update._mode_note: satellite autoUpgrades from a pinned/signed ref; tappaas-cicd never SSHes in to push (ADR-010 §7.3)
